text: A well-designed REST API is essential for creating a user-friendly experience. First, use clear and logical endpoints that represent resources, like `/users` or `/orders`, to keep things intuitive. Next, always use appropriate status codes; for example, `200` for success, `400` for bad requests, and `404` for not found. This helps developers understand what’s happening. Versioning is also important. By adding a version number, like `/v1/users`, you can make changes without breaking existing clients. Finally, provide thorough documentation that explains each endpoint, its parameters, and expected responses. This makes your API easy to integrate and saves developers time.
